articles.title: Two Lebanese paramedics wounded in landmine explosion
articles.summary: Two Lebanese paramedics wounded in landmine explosion The Lebanese Red Cross said on Tuesday that one of its ambulances was hit by a landmine explosion while conducting a rescue mission in the Zawtar al-Sharqiya area, damaging the ambulance and injuring two paramedics. In a statement, the Red Cross added that it was “working in coordination with the Lebanese Army’s Engineering Regiment and relevant authorities to secure the area, which has been found to contain mines and unexploded ordnance, to allow safe access to paramedics and their evacuation”.

articles.summary: Netherlands to ban goods from Israeli settlements The Dutch government announced on Tuesday that it will ban products from Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories starting on 22 September. articles.title: US announces trade deal with Jordan
articles.summary: US announces trade deal with Jordan The US announced on Tuesday that they will be entering a trade deal with Jordan which would remove barriers for US exporters and provide new access for critical US industries.  According to a statement from the White House, Jordan will remove non-tariff trade barriers and expand market access for US goods, including agricultural products and motor vehicles, as part of the deal. This comes amid renewed Iranian strikes on Jordan, which killed two US military personnel on Friday.
